source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A square with side length $30 \mathrm{~cm}$ is split into 9 squares. The big square contains three circles with radii $5 \mathrm{~cm}$ (bottom right), $4 \mathrm{~cm}$ (top left) as well as $3 \mathrm{~cm}$ (top right) as seen in the diagram. How many $\mathrm{cm}^{2}$ are shaded in grey?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'as seen in the diagram,' so the square partition and circle placement depend on the image.
The arrangement of 9 squares and the positions of the three circles are not fully recoverable from text alone.
multi_step: Solving likely requires combining the 30 cm outer square, the 9-square partition, and three circle radii.
The task appears to involve geometric decomposition/area subtraction rather than a single direct lookup.
Image-grounded reasoning is needed to infer how the partition sizes relate to the circles and shaded region.
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='500', which is easy to compare against a final computed result.
The geometry setup suggests a determinate area answer that can be checked by a derived solution.
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and the image asset exists, supporting downstream verification.

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, image, and numeric answer are all present.
Asset registry passed with no recorded issues, and the primary image exists.
Although the geometry is underspecified in text alone, the required diagram is available, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scores indicate strong image dependency, good multi-step reasoning value, and high verifiability.
